title: selaginella pygmaea
intro: Selaginella pygmaea, also known as the dwarf spike moss, is a small, non-vascular plant species that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family, characterized by its small size and spiky appearance. Traditionally, Selaginella pygmaea has been used in herbal medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Selaginella pygmaea is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are used in traditional herbal remedies.
